result:
**Queue**: point-to-point. One message → one consumer. Use for work distribution (task queues, job processing) where each message must be processed exactly once by one worker. Multiple consumers compete; load balances across them.

**Topic**: pub/sub. One message → many subscribers. Use for broadcasting events where multiple independent systems need the same data (order placed → billing, inventory, analytics all react).

Rule of thumb:
- "Do this work" → queue
- "This happened" → topic

Hybrid (e.g., Kafka consumer groups, SNS→SQS fanout) gives you topic semantics with per-subscriber queue durability — use when each subscriber needs its own independent, replayable stream.
